BacktestRequest.description:
POST /api/backtest request -- the unified `{run, legs}` contract (U3).
Replaces the pre-U3 scalar shape and the separate /api/backtest/compare
endpoint: 1 leg is a plain backtest, 2+ legs is a comparison, and a
`benchmark: true` leg or explicit `run.reference` produces per-leg
`relative` metrics.
BacktestRequest.title: BacktestRequest

ExecutionConfig.description: Execution, costs, risk, sizing, timing — all in one.
ExecutionConfig.title: ExecutionConfig

LatestSignalRequest.description:
POST /api/latest-signal request -- inherently single-strategy (there
is no P&L or cross-leg comparison to unify), so it keeps the pre-U3
scalar shape rather than the {run, legs} contract used by /api/backtest.
LatestSignalRequest.title: LatestSignalRequest

LegSpec.description:
One leg of a /api/backtest run -- a plain strategy, a precomputed- signals leg, or a `benchmark: true` preset (a forced always-long, zero-cost, no-stops baseline). 1..20 legs per request; 1 leg = a plain backtest with identical semantics to the pre-U3 scalar shape.

Structural / cross-leg validation (uniqueness, benchmark-forbidden fields, exposure bounds, reference resolution, period / signal_frequency agreement) happens in the endpoint handler so every violation gets its own 400 `code` -- see `_validate_and_prepare_legs`.
LegSpec.title: LegSpec

OHLCVInput.description: Inline OHLCV data as parallel arrays.
OHLCVInput.title: OHLCVInput

RunSpec.description: Run-level overrides shared across every leg of a /api/backtest call.
RunSpec.title: RunSpec

StatsTradeInput.description: Single trade record for /api/stats. Accepts the same shape as run_result.trades produced by run_backtest.
StatsTradeInput.title: StatsTradeInput

StrategyInput.description:
Strategy definition — condition_tree + indicators.

`condition_tree` is a json-logic-style bundle with four nullable slots (long_entry / long_exit / short_entry / short_exit), each a tree of and/or/not/leaf nodes. `indicators` is an ordered DAG of ResolvedIndicator dicts referenced by tree leaves via `ref`.

A key the strategy schema does not declare — at the top level, inside an `indicators[]` entry, or inside `condition_tree` — is rejected with `422 UNKNOWN_FIELD` rather than ignored.
StrategyInput.title: StrategyInput

TimeSeriesInput.description: Non-OHLCV time-series input for model indicators (parallel arrays).
TimeSeriesInput.title: TimeSeriesInput

ValidateStrategyRequest.description:
Request body for POST /api/validate-strategy.

The strategy document must be nested under the `strategy` key. Requests missing `strategy`, or carrying unknown top-level fields (e.g. the document's own fields sent at the top level), are rejected with a 422 naming the offending fields.

injected_indicators.description: Names of custom indicators the caller will supply as data_inputs at run time. Added to the column allow-list for condition-tree validation without requiring registry entries.
injected_indicators.title: Injected Indicators

strategy.description: Strategy document (condition_tree + indicators, or precomputed_signals). Required — requests without it are rejected.
strategy.title: Strategy

ValidateStrategyRequest.title: ValidateStrategyRequest

context.description: Extra detail per error code (e.g. unknown column names).
context.title: Context

location.description: JSON-Pointer into the offending node, e.g. /condition_tree/long_entry.
location.title: Location

ValidationFailure.description: 422 body returned when a strategy/indicator document fails validation.
ValidationFailure.title: ValidationFailure

post.description:
Run 1..20 legs and return the unified ``{run, legs}`` response.

A single leg behaves exactly like a plain backtest. 2+ legs is a comparison; a benchmark: true leg (or an explicit run.reference) adds a relative block (Alpha/Beta/IR/Tracking Error/Up-Down Capture) to every OTHER leg.

**Premium features.** The following require an upgraded subscription; requests using them without one return `403 PLAN_UPGRADE_REQUIRED`:

- Server-side symbol fetch — otherwise inline OHLCV data must be supplied.

- The full statistic set — otherwise a 20-statistic summary is returned.

Re-run 1 to 20 legs using the same request body as /api/backtest and return a multi-sheet Excel workbook instead of a JSON response: a Comparison sheet (headline stats per leg plus the alignment report), a Relative Metrics sheet when a reference leg is present, and per-leg Statistics/Trades/Drawdowns/Equity+Returns/Market Data sheets. run.response_detail and run.stats_keys do not apply to this endpoint (there is no JSON body to trim or re-key).

**Premium features.** The following require an upgraded subscription; requests using them without one return `403 PLAN_UPGRADE_REQUIRED`:

- Server-side symbol fetch — otherwise inline OHLCV data must be supplied.

**Premium access.** This endpoint requires an upgraded subscription; requests without one return `403 SCOPE_DENIED`.

Same ``{run, legs}`` request shape as /api/backtest — returns dummy
per-leg results instantly.

get.description:
Return the server-wide bar-frequency capability set plus the hard signal-by-execution compatibility matrix.

`valid_execution_bars` maps each signal frequency to the execution bar frequencies the engine supports for it; only the listed (signal_frequency, execution_bar_frequency) pairs are valid. `open_hour`/`close_hour` are meaningful in exactly one case, flagged by `open_close_hour_applies_when`: a daily signal with hourly execution.

Return valid execution anchors and fill algorithms.

Args:
    is_24h: Whether the market trades 24h (e.g., crypto). Default True.
    anchor: If provided, return valid fill algorithms for this anchor only.

Return indicator registry with parameter schemas + strategy-logic metadata.

Extended to expose `kind`, `arity`, `value_dtype`, `output_columns`, `tags`, plus a top-level `version` SHA-256 over registry contents for client cache invalidation.

The optional `descriptions=false` query param omits the per-entry `description` field (additive; default-true keeps the response shape).

parameters.description: Include the per-indicator `description` field. Default true (unchanged response). Set false to omit `description` from every entry — halves the payload for callers that don't need it; all other fields are unchanged.
parameters.name: descriptions

schema.description: Include the per-indicator `description` field. Default true (unchanged response). Set false to omit `description` from every entry — halves the payload for callers that don't need it; all other fields are unchanged.
schema.title: Descriptions

post.description:
Return the strategy's latest signal for the most recent bar of OHLCV.

Single-strategy only — there is no P&L or cross-leg comparison to unify, so this endpoint keeps its own single-run request shape rather than the multi-leg comparison shape /api/backtest uses.

Returns only the latest bar's signal value plus the latest indicator panels — no P&L, statistics, or equity curve. Use it to check what the strategy would signal right now (e.g. for live trading).

**Premium features.** The following require an upgraded subscription; requests using them without one return `403 PLAN_UPGRADE_REQUIRED`:

- Server-side symbol fetch — otherwise inline OHLCV data must be supplied.

get.description:
Introspect the calling API key: the permission scopes it carries, its numeric limits, current usage against those limits, and capability flags.

Any valid key can call this — a key can always see its own permissions and limits. Usage counts include this request itself, consistent with the X-RateLimit-* headers on the same response. `max_bars_per_run` is null when the key has no bar cap; `usage` mirrors the rate-limit headers and adds the concurrency picture, which has no header.
Return condition operators for building strategy logic.
Return the JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) for the Strategy document.

Return section → field groupings for the performance stats, plus the stable metric catalog.

Each section carries `fields_summary` and `fields_full` arrays listing the display labels currently present in the `stats` dict, in render order. `metrics` is the flat catalog mapping each metric's permanent snake_case `id` to its current display label.

Do not key on the section field labels — they are mutable and may be reworded. Key on `metrics[].id` (stable, never changes) and use the section arrays only for grouping and order; render `metrics[].label` for display.
Return position sizing methods with parameters.

post.description:
Compute performance metrics from a return series.

**Premium features.** The following require an upgraded subscription; requests using them without one return `403 PLAN_UPGRADE_REQUIRED`:

- The full statistic set — otherwise a 20-statistic summary is returned.

get.description: Return stop loss types with per-type parameters.

422.description: Document failed validation. Each error carries a machine-readable `code` and a JSON-Pointer `location` into the offending node.

post.description:
Validate a Strategy document without running a backtest.

Body shape:
    {
      "strategy": <Strategy doc>,
      "scope": {"leaf_paths": [...]},     # optional — validate only these leaves
      "injected_indicators": ["name1"]    # optional — column names the caller
                                          # supplies as data_inputs at run time
    }

injected_indicators names additional columns that will be available at run time (as inline data_inputs series) but are not registered indicators. The validator treats them as available columns so condition_tree leaves referencing them pass; they are not checked against the indicator registry.

Returns 200 with {valid: true, warmup_bars, referenced_indicators,
referenced_columns_per_slot, warnings: []} on success, or 422 with
{valid: false, errors: [...]} on failure. Each error carries a structured
`code` and a JSON-Pointer `location`.

422.description: Document failed validation. Each error carries a machine-readable `code` and a JSON-Pointer `location` into the offending node.

get.description: Return engine version and API contract number.

tags.description: Introspection for the calling API key. Any valid key — no particular plan required.
tags.name: Account

tags.description: Market data access and file parsing.
tags.name: Data
